text: Based on the geological environment survey data of the coastal zone from 2018 to 2022, the coastline evolution model and the seabed erosion and siltation model were respectively established to analyze and predict the characteristics of coastline evolution and seabed erosion and siltation. The results show that in recent years, the coastal erosion of Yazhou Bay has mainly been concentrated at the mouth of the Yanzao River and the eastern section of the breakwater of the Yazhou Central Fishing Port. Coastal siltation is concentrated in the wave shadow area of Moon Island (artificial island) and the coastline section of Yazhou Bay Science and Technology City. The prediction of shoreline evolution shows that in 20 years, the maximum erosion distance of the Yazhou Bay shoreline will be approximately 33 meters, located on the east side of the Yanzao River estuary. The maximum siltation distance of the shoreline is approximately 99 meters, located on the west side of the approach bridge on Moon Island. The prediction of erosion and siltation changes shows that the maximum erosion intensity of the seabed is approximately 0.14 m/a, located in the waters outside the Moon Island. The maximum siltation intensity is approximately 0.07 m/a and it is located in the nearshore waters of the Science and Technology City. The main reason is that the construction of the Moon Island, the Central Fishing Port and the Nanshan Port has changed the local sediment discharge direction, and at the same time caused changes in the hydrodynamic conditions, thereby affecting the overall erosion and siltation pattern.

To explore the dynamics of coastal erosion and its prevention and control measures, a time series of the tidal flat area in Yancheng Nature Reserve was created using the Landsat series remote sensing image dataset from 1985 to 2023. The coastal silting and erosion dynamics, changes in land use types and other indicators in different years were analyzed. The results show that: (1) From 1985 to 2023, the Yancheng Nature Reserve presented a development trend of transforming from a siltation coast (1988-2012) to an erosion coast (2012-2023). (2) Coastal siltation mainly showed a gradually slowing trend, reaching its peak at a rate of 521.8 hm2·a-1 from 1997 to 2000. Coastal erosion showed a trend of weakening first and then strengthening. From 2003 to 2009, coastal erosion was the slowest, with a rate of 0.7 hm2·a-1. (3) The area of wetlands and Spartina alterniflora wetlands affected by human activities in the study area gradually increased at an average annual rate of 501.4 and 150.2 hm2·a-1, respectively. (4) The Spartina alterniflora communities and human activity areas along the coast accounted for 69.7% and 13.0% of the total silted area, respectively. The net eroded Spartina alterniflora communities and human activity areas along the coast accounted for 34.4% and 22.7% of the total eroded area, respectively. Spartina alterniflora has played a significant role in promoting siltation and protecting beaches in Yancheng Nature Reserve. However, in terms of prevention and control measures, it is necessary to actively strengthen the early warning mechanism and explore the optimal Yancheng solution for dealing with coastal erosion.

Coastline erosion prediction is one of the hot issues in the study of coastal dynamic geomorphology. This paper, based on the Long Short-Term Neural Memory Network (LSTM), uses the shoreline, water depth and intertidal zone width data collected in the sea area near Sheyang County, Jiangsu Province from 1985 to 2023, combined with the wave and tidal current data retrieved from ERA5 data, to construct a coastal erosion risk prediction model. This prediction model can accurately reflect the nonlinear or linear changing trends of accelerated erosion, stable erosion and siltation of the coastline. The prediction results show that under the condition of reduced sand sources, the strengthening of waves and tidal currents has been the main factor of coastal erosion in the Sheyang Sea area in the past 20 years. In addition, experiments on the protective effects of coastal reinforcement, wave dissipation and weak current on the coast were carried out using predictive models. The test results show that coastal reinforcement has the best protective effect, while wave dissipation has a better protective effect than weak current. The operation process of the predictive model setting is highly efficient, and it has high application value and development potential.

Coastal erosion is a complex hydrodynamic phenomenon, resulting from the interaction of natural forces such as waves, water currents and tides, as well as human factors like urban renovation and climate change. Understanding this process is of vital importance for the sustainable development management of coastal areas, especially for reducing the ecological risks brought about by infrastructure construction and environmental restoration. In this study, we focused on two main issues: one involving trapezoidal coastal structures and the other having a simple rectangular shape. By using the advanced functions of Ansys Fluent, numerical simulations were carried out to simulate the interaction between wave patterns and different coastal structures, thereby better understanding and predicting coastal erosion under various conditions. The Ansys Fluent software uses the Navier-Stokes equations and the k-ωSST turbulence model to capture the interaction between fluid dynamics and wave structure. The fluid volume element (VOF) model is also used to handle air-water interface problems. The simulation influencing factors include variables such as wave height, speed, pressure, turbulent kinetic energy and wall shear stress, which are applied to different wave conditions and forces, such as wave shape, wave height, inclination Angle, direction of action and coastal structure. In the study, these variables were also monitored at different points A, B, C and D, ranging from the lowest point to the highest point. The main conclusions are as follows: (1) At the lowest monitoring point A of the coastal structure, the highest pressure and the highest wave were recorded, indicating that the coastal structure was subjected to a significant load. Point B is an intermediate point that records the highest wall shear stress and highlights the strong interaction between the waves and the coastal structure. At high point C, the turbulent kinetic energy is significant and the water flow velocity is high, indicating an increased risk of erosion at this point. (2) Due to the direct impact of waves, the low-angle tilt causes the wave height to increase. Meanwhile, the significant increase in wave height at the corner indicates that the direction of the waves and the structural analysis of the structures in the coastal structure design are of great significance. (3) At the corners, the increase in pressure has a significant impact, so it is necessary to reinforce the coastal structures to withstand the more intense hydrodynamic effects. (4) The shape of waves has a significant impact on the flow velocity of water. There is a significant difference between shape F1 and shape F2, and the average flow velocity generated by shape F1 is higher. (5) The influence of different waveforms on the shear stress of the wall surface also varies significantly. The shear stress induced by the F1 waveform is higher than that of the F2 waveform. A moderate inclination Angle is also conducive to higher shear force. (6) The higher the wave height, the greater the turbulent kinetic energy. Sharp turns can also intensify this energy, thereby affecting erosion and deposition. Coastal structures like SC1 also enhance this energy by interacting with natural wave dynamics. Restore

The delta is a zone where land and sea meet, and it is also a dense area where human economic activities are vigorous. Under the influence of various disaster-causing factors such as sediment reduction in river basins, rising sea levels and frequent storm events, delta erosion has intensified, exerting serious negative impacts on the socio-economic system. To address the erosion of delta coasts and enhance the capacity for disaster prevention and mitigation, the risk assessment of delta coast erosion is of vital importance. At present, coastal erosion assessment mostly focuses on risk research at a single scale, concentrating on individual elements of risk, such as hazard assessment, vulnerability assessment or exposure assessment of disaster-bearing bodies. It is extremely limited to conduct risk assessment of delta coastal erosion at different temporal and spatial scales from the perspective of overall risk. This study constructed a risk assessment framework for delta coastal erosion at different spatial scales from the global scale, regional scale and local scale, and carried out case studies on the risk assessment of coastal erosion in typical large river deltas. From the perspective of time scale, a risk assessment process for erosion of delta sandy coasts under the superimposed scenario of future sea level rise and storm events was proposed, providing an important reference for conducting the assessment of the impact of climate change on coastal erosion. Studies show that the proportion of high and extremely dangerous coastal erosion in the Yangtze River Delta accounts for 36%. The high and extremely dangerous shoreline sections located outside the Yangtze River estuary are greatly affected by Marine dynamics, while the shoreline sections within the Yangtze River estuary are caused by a combination of Marine dynamics and sediment content in the water body, resulting in high and extremely dangerous ones. High and extremely vulnerable areas are mainly distributed along the coasts of Chongming Island and Haimen City. The eastern coast of Chongming Island is dominated by social vulnerability, while its western coast and the southern coast of Haimen City have narrow tidal flats, which is the result of the combined effect of natural vulnerability and social vulnerability. High and extremely high exposure levels are distributed in the shoreline sections with high population density and high land use value, located in Taicang City, Jiangsu Province and the main land area of Shanghai except the southern part of Pudong New Area and Fengxian District. The risk levels of coastal erosion in the Yangtze River Delta are mainly medium and low, accounting for 71.12%. The risk levels of coastal erosion along the southern branch, Nangang and Hangzhou Bay are high, and measures such as reinforcing embankments and building ecological sea walls can be adopted for protection. Studies show that currently, the areas with high and extremely high erosion vulnerability are located along the coastline from Wusongkou to Sanjia Port, accounting for 31.3%. By 2035, the length of high and extremely vulnerable shoreline sections increased by 16.4%, mainly located in the upstream shoreline sections of Pudong Airport and Sanjia Port. According to the 2035 territorial space master plan of Pudong New Area, the area from south of the Dazhi River to Hangzhou Bay has been converted from agricultural land and unused land to tourism and urban construction land. The high and extremely high exposure bank sections have increased by 20.5%, especially in the area around Nanhuizui and Yangshan Port Free Trade Zone. With the future reduction of sand in the basin and the development of Pudong New Area, the number of assessment units with extremely high risk levels has increased by 17%. The length of the shoreline sections with extremely high erosion vulnerability and extremely high land use exposure has increased to 47.5 kilometers, and the proportion has risen to 45.8%. The risk levels in the areas of Pudong Airport and Luchao Port on the northern shore of Hangzhou Bay have increased, and it is necessary to strengthen erosion monitoring and coastal protection.

Based on the analysis of the erosion characteristics of the muddy coast in Jiangsu, the development process of coastal protection projects in Jiangsu was sorted out. With the southward shift of the erosion and siltation transition node, the range of eroded coastline sections along the Jiangsu coast has gradually expanded, with the beach surface eroded and a large area of coastal salt marshes disappearing. The traditional forms of local coastal protection have become passive, and there is an urgent need for a sustainable coastal erosion management strategy that combines local and macro aspects, as well as nature and engineering. It is pointed out that the banks of the abandoned Yellow River Delta should be regarded as a whole, and a macroscopic overall coastal protection system combining 'large dikes + offshore dikes' should be established. Longitudinally, construct a coastline with alternating capes and bays as nodes using artificial capes. Horizontally, the restoration and reconstruction of salt marsh ecosystems are carried out through the use of offshore dikes to reduce waves and promote siltation.

By using the methods of shore profile elevation monitoring, sediment grain size change and dynamic environment analysis, taking the beach erosion evolution process in Haiyang City as an example, the erosion characteristics and influencing factors of the typical sandy coast in the eastern part of the Shandong Peninsula were analyzed. The results show that the beaches in Haiyang City are mainly eroded as a whole, and the total erosion volume of the beaches from 2018 to 2019 was 2.48×10 ~ 4m3. Among them, the T-shaped mouth beach is mainly eroded, but due to the influence of the estuary on both sides, there is a phenomenon of local beach surface stability and weak siltation at the back of the beach. The erosion of the beach surface at Chaoli - Zhuangshang - Yangjiaopan Beach gradually intensifies from west to east. The middle part of the 10,000-meter beach is severely eroded, while the two ends are relatively less eroded. The surface sediments gradually become thinner from land to sea, but overall they show a trend of coarsening. Waves and extreme weather are important dynamic factors controlling the erosion changes of beaches, while slope is one of the significant factors leading to the morphological changes of beach profiles and the differences in surface sediments.

Coastal erosion leads to land loss, seriously threatening people's lives and property. Identifying the vulnerability of coastal erosion is of great significance for disaster prevention and mitigation. The evaluation index system was constructed from three aspects: coastal dynamics, coastal morphology and socio-economic. By using the digital shoreline analysis system (DSAS) and remote sensing data, the coast was dispersed into evaluation units with equal spacing by the section method. Based on the entropy weight method, the weights and grades of evaluation indicators are determined and the coastal erosion vulnerability is calculated. The spatial differentiation and influencing factors of coastal erosion vulnerability are identified by using geographic detectors. The results show that The proportions of extremely vulnerable, highly vulnerable, moderately vulnerable, low-vulnerable and very low-vulnerable coastal erosion vulnerability in central Jiangsu Province are 5.60%, 15.80%, 30.93%, 24.21% and 23.46% respectively. The overall distribution trend of coastal erosion vulnerability is higher in the north and lower in the south. Among them, the extremely vulnerable area is mainly located in the coastal zone between the Zhongshan Estuary and the Sheyang Estuary. The spatial differentiation of coastal erosion vulnerability in central Jiangsu is the result of the combined effect of multiple factors such as coastal dynamics, coastal morphology, and socio-economic conditions. Among them, tidal flat slope, surface cover type, average tidal range, and coastline change rate are the dominant factors for the spatial differentiation of coastal erosion vulnerability.

Due to the active sediment movement on sandy coasts, the shoreline near buildings that protrude into the sea will undergo significant changes after construction. To facilitate further research on the downstream erosion problems and protective measures of buildings on sandy coasts, this paper reviews the above content based on previous research results. Based on several typical engineering cases such as the Port of Friendship in Mauritania, the coastal change patterns downstream of sandy coastal structures are analyzed from the aspects of shoreline changes and profile changes. This paper explores the water and sediment movement mechanisms near three protective measures, namely, the spur dike, the outflow dike and the offshore submerged dike, and reviews the two soft protective measures, namely, artificial beach maintenance and bypass sediment transport. Finally, the future research directions of coastal erosion downstream of sandy coastal structures and corresponding protective measures are prospected.

The coastal zone is an active area where land and sea interact. Due to its superior geographical location, it has become the most active and concentrated region for human activities. With the global rise in sea levels and the intensification of human exploitation and utilization, coastal erosion and coastline retreat have gradually become the main threats to the survival and development of coastal areas. Studying the vulnerability of coastal erosion under the influence of current climate change and human activities is of great significance to the safety and development of coastal cities, coastal protection, and the conservation of the natural environment. This study takes the Coastal zone of Hainan Island as the research object and adopts CERA(Coastal Erosion Risk Assessment) and CVI-PCA(Coastal Vulnerability Index-Principal Components) Two assessment models were used to evaluate its vulnerability to coastal erosion. CVI-PCA is a new index evaluation system proposed based on the assessment results of CERA and the measured data of beaches, and compares and analyzes the main influencing factors of coastal erosion and the assessment results of the two methods. The shoreline change trend and the coping strategies for coastal erosion under the RCP(Representative Concentration Pathway)4.5 scenario were discussed. The following conclusions are drawn: 1. Risk assessment of coastal erosion in Hainan Island based on CERA. The coastal areas of Hainan Island are urban and densely populated, with a high sensitivity to coastal erosion, showing a high degree of erosion vulnerability. The high-risk areas are all distributed in the eastern region, with the most significant erosion risks in the eastern side of Wulong Port in Wenchang, the sections along the coast of Coconut Grove Bay and Yalong Bay in Sanya. Topography, population density, distance from the shoreline and the rate of shoreline change are the main contribution indicators of the sensitivity, value, exposure and coastal erosion modules respectively. Meanwhile, topography contributes the most to vulnerability and consequences, and the rate of shoreline change contributes the most to risk. The coastal characteristics and erosion results of Hainan Island were analyzed, and it was found that the CERA model is applicable. Some improvements can also be made. For instance, the continuously rising sea level will lead to a gradual increase in the distance of coastal retreat. The influence coefficient of sea level change in the evaluation model is set at 0.1, and it is believed that appropriate adjustments can be made to demonstrate its significant role. Meanwhile, coarse-grained sediments have a certain blocking effect on coastal erosion. It is advisable to incorporate sediment particle size into the coastal erosion risk assessment system and fully consider the spatial differences of indicators to enhance refined research. 2. The assessment of coastal erosion vulnerability of Hainan Island based on CVI-PCA selected five principal components for analysis, and the cumulative contribution rate was 75%. It was found that the average particle size, significant wave height, and tidal range have a strong correlation with the principal component, while the slope, population density, and per capita GDP are only strongly correlated with a single principal component. The coast of Hainan Island mainly shows a high grade of erosion vulnerability (62.67%), followed by a medium grade of erosion vulnerability (37.22%). Extremely vulnerable areas are distributed within the lagoon of Puqian Bay. High vulnerability is mainly distributed in the eastern and northern regions. All other county and city areas are moderately vulnerable. High-vulnerability areas are characterized by openness, large particle size, fully reflective beaches, small tidal range, gentle slope, and large receding distance of the shoreline due to changes in sea level. 3. Comparison of the results of Coastal erosion vulnerability assessment and Management Strategies: The assessment results of the two models are analyzed from four aspects: evaluation methods, evaluation indicators, spatial distribution, and influencing factors. In terms of evaluation indicators, both methods take into account the influencing factors such as coastal conditions, dynamics, and socio-economic aspects. The CVI method incorporates more measured data indicators, such as average particle size and beach condition types. The two storm surge (storm level increase and quantity) and sea level change indicators of CERA were replaced by the receding distance of the shoreline, and some adjustments were made. The vulnerability assessment results of the two methods are basically the same. The high-vulnerability areas are mainly distributed in the eastern and northern regions and Sanya City. Geomorphology, the rate of shoreline change and the distance of storm/coastal retreat are the main influencing factors of coastal erosion. The Digital shoreline analysis system (DSAS) is adopted to analyze and predict the position changes and change rates of the shoreline. From 2010 to 2081, the coastline of Hainan Island was in an erosive state, and the rate of change was mainly at a medium to low level (-1.5 m/a to -0.5 m/a). Within a certain spatial scale, the position of the shoreline changes relatively little, and the changes mainly occur in the northeastern and southwestern parts. Obvious siltation occurred along the shoreline sections such as Shatu Bay, Jinsha Bay, Dadonghai, Dun Tou Bay and Yangpu Bay, while erosion took place at Puqian Bay and the estuary of the Changhua River. The impact of a 1 m/a beach change on coastal resources and other aspects still cannot be underestimated. Scientific measures should be taken to reduce the disaster risk of coastal erosion. Currently, severely eroded sections such as Sanya Bay, Yudai Beach, and Bangtang Bay mostly adopt beach maintenance or a combination of soft and hard methods for rectification and restoration. In the future, beach feeding can be the main approach, supplemented by biological measures and hard engineering. The vulnerability and resilience of the coast should be comprehensively evaluated to formulate appropriate strategies.

Coastal erosion is widely distributed around the world, causing huge losses to the production and life of people along the coast. Based on previous related studies and the author's research results in the Shandong Peninsula and the Yellow River Delta, this paper analyzes the spatio-temporal variation laws of coastal erosion and the research on protection technologies in recent years. The results show that, first of all, coastal erosion is an adaptive adjustment and rebalancing process of coastal dynamic geomorphology, which has significant spatial differences and temporal nonlinear characteristics. Secondly, the influencing factors of coastal erosion are significantly diverse, and the erosion mechanism is significantly regional. Finally, the protection and restoration of coastal erosion should focus on the sustainability of the effect. On the basis of in-depth research and clarification of the evolution trend of regional coastal dynamic geomorphology, 'soft' coastal protection technologies and new coastal protection structures should be developed and applied.

Conducting an assessment of coastal erosion vulnerability on Hainan Island is of great significance for the protection of ecological resources and disaster prevention in coastal areas. The coastal vulnerability model in the comprehensive assessment model of ecosystem services and trade-offs was adopted to synthesize the Exposure Index (EI) to evaluate the coastal erosion vulnerability of Hainan Island. For typical study areas, an evaluation system of coastal characteristics - coastal dynamics - economic and social indicators was established. The preferred evaluation factors of coastal zone characteristics included coastal erosion rate, coastal type, coastal habitat, etc. Further, the vulnerability index was quantified by using the comprehensive index method. Eventually, the vulnerability of the coastal areas of Hainan Island under different scenarios, as well as the coastal erosion rate and erosion vulnerability level of key areas, were obtained. The research results show that: (1) The spatial distribution of erosion vulnerability in Hainan Island is lower in the east and higher in the west. Among them, the vulnerability of cities and counties in the southwest is the highest, that of cities and counties in the southeast is the lowest, and the vulnerability of the remaining areas is moderate. The coastal vulnerability in no-habitat scenarios is much higher than that in habitat conservation scenarios. (2) In the typical study area, the sandy coasts on the east and west coasts of Haikou suffered more erosion from 2016 to 2020, with the maximum area exceeding 20 m/a. The coastal erosion vulnerability of the main urban areas such as Longhua and Meilan in Haikou City is high, followed by the west coast and the east coast respectively, and the Dongzhaigang area has the lowest vulnerability. (3) Research has found that coastlines protected by habitats such as mangroves can be effectively protected with extremely low vulnerability, while degraded sandy shorelines show higher vulnerability. Therefore, it is necessary to protect the coastal habitat and prevent the loss of coastal sediment.

Under the dual pressure of global climate change and high-intensity human development, coastal erosion and habitat deterioration in densely populated and economically developed coastal areas have become problems that cannot be ignored. The Pearl River Estuary Greater Bay Area is located on the southeast coast of China. It has a low-lying terrain and is frequently hit by typhoons. Meanwhile, the reform and opening up have led to rapid economic development, a sharp increase in population, continuous expansion of land towards the sea, and frequent renovations of the coastline. Hard artificial shoreline and high-value coastal buildings pose a huge erosion risk challenge to the Bay Area's coastal zone system. To prevent further harm from coastal erosion, this study collected the data of coastal erosion vulnerability factors in this area and analyzed their characteristics. By using the factor weighting method of AHP- independence weight method, the ESA framework and TOPSIS method as the evaluation methods of coastal erosion vulnerability, and the data analysis methods of Jenks method and obstacle degree model, the results of reform and opening up were concluded The spatial characteristics, evolution process and main driving factors of coastal erosion and exposure, sensitivity and adaptability in the bay area. This study developed a set of methods applicable to evaluating the vulnerability of coastal erosion in the Pearl River Estuary Greater Bay Area and aims to extend it to similar areas to provide scientific guidance for coastal management and planning. The main research is as follows: (1) This study selected 75 evaluation units from 15 coastal counties and districts in the Pearl River Estuary Greater Bay Area over five periods from 1980 to 2020. Based on natural features and human activities, 12 factors affecting the vulnerability of coastal erosion were selected from five elements: coastal natural features, hydrodynamic conditions, economy, population and shoreline changes as evaluation indicators, and they were classified into three types of elements: exposure, sensitivity and adaptability of the coastal zone system. (2) The spatio-temporal distribution characteristics of coastal erosion vulnerability are obvious. The study area shows the characteristics of low coastal erosion vulnerability in the eastern hilly region and higher vulnerability in the central and western delta plain region. The natural features of the coast are the main influencing factors. The central area is low-lying, with weak storm surges and wave effects, a large tidal range, and relatively poor coastal protection compared to the east and west sides. The value of coastal buildings is relatively high. After the reform and opening up, the study area developed rapidly, with economic growth showing an exponential increase. The population continuously gathered in developed areas, and the length of artificial shorelines and the area of land reclamation kept rising. After 2010, human shoreline renovation activities in the coastal areas of the Greater Bay Area eased somewhat, and the coastal protection capacity began to improve. (3) The Pearl River Estuary Greater Bay Area has experienced the era of coastal erosion where economic development coexisted with the deterioration of coastal zone vulnerability from 1980 to 2010, and the era of coastal recovery under policy guidance from 2010 to 2020. Economic factors have been the main influencing factors. Although the coastal zone recovered later compared with other bay areas in the world, thanks to its efficient policies, it has demonstrated the advantage of catching up from behind, and the duration of coastal zone damage is relatively short. (4) To prevent the further intensification of coastal erosion vulnerability in the Pearl River Estuary Greater Bay Area, a prevention and control strategy should be adopted, mainly by reducing the exposure of the coastal zone system and enhancing its adaptability, and supplemented by formulating measures to deal with secondary Marine disasters caused by sea level rise and frequent extreme weather.

The terrain along the coast of the beach area is relatively uniform, with decreasing elevation from the shore to the sea. The zonation is obvious, and in the case of unified and holistic terrain monitoring data, the method of comparing the inversion results of fixed sections and remote sensing water edge lines is adopted, providing the scouring and siltation performance of the beach in both horizontal and vertical directions, offering a relatively comprehensive basis for understanding the scouring and siltation characteristics of the beach. The eastern section of Sanya Bay, under the cover of Luhuitou Peninsula on the southeast side, has relatively weak dynamic conditions, and beach erosion is mainly due to the reduction of sediment sources caused by partial filling and occupation of the original sand dam, the formation of artificial revetments, and the construction of artificial islands and other projects, leading to local dynamic enhancement. Increasing the effective supply of sediment and enhancing the beach's resistance to erosion are necessary measures for restoring and protecting the shoreline. The beach is located in the tourism and leisure area of Sanya City. Physical structures such as the spur dike and the off-bank embankment, as well as adjustments caused by erosion and siltation of the shoreline, have a certain adverse impact on the landscape and leisure functions of Sanya Bay. Beach maintenance measures that do not alter the natural attributes of the shoreline, similar to coastal restoration and maintenance, are more appropriate options. Selecting a sand source that is coarser than the original beach sand can enhance the anti-scouring capacity of the beach, but the slope of the beach formed by overly coarse sand is steeper, which affects pedestrians. A sand source slightly coarser than the original beach sand is more suitable for maintaining the stability and recreational functions of the beach. Practical experience shows that the form, range, scale, and sand of beach shoulder replenishment, designed in combination with the existing beach shoreline and terrain pattern, is suitable and effective for restoring the increasingly serious beach erosion in the eastern section of Sanya Bay, largely restoring the original beach landscape of the coastline section. Based on regular monitoring and research after sand replenishment, determining the reasonable timing of re-replenishment is important to further maintain the beach landscape and leisure functions of the eastern section of Sanya Bay and provides a good reference for restoring and protecting similar tourist and leisure beaches.

Seawalls are important protective barriers for social and economic activities in coastal areas. The intensification of coastal erosion will lead to a decrease in the stability and safety of seawalls, increase the risk of extreme storm floods in coastal areas, and thereby affect the safety of coastal areas. This paper selects the area of Shanghai Petrochemical, which is severely affected by coastal erosion, as the research area. GIS is used to analyze the erosion characteristics of the nearshore seabed from 1972 to 2020, and the stability of the nearshore seawall of Shanghai Petrochemical is evaluated based on the variation of -5 m isobars from 2000 to 2020. The results show that from 1972 to 2020, the seabed at the front of Shanghai Petrochemical was mainly eroded as a whole. The shallow beach erosion on the east and west sides of the nearshore area of the petrochemical was obvious. The stability of the middle section of the urban beach, the 6th embankment and the seawall on the east bank of the wharf was the lowest. Based on the above research results, considering the scenarios of extreme storm floods overbreaking or collapsing in the weak stability sections of the seawall, the risks of Shanghai Petrochemical encountering once-in-a-millennium extreme storm floods in 2010, 2030 and 2050 were simulated and analyzed. The results show that under the base year scenario of 2010, the urban beaches and the 6th dike area, which are most affected by coastal erosion, have the highest risk of encountering extreme storm floods. By 2050, the safety of the currently relatively stable sea dike will also be greatly reduced. Compared with 2010, the direct economic losses of the coastal areas of Shanghai Petrochemical will increase nearly threefold.

Global climate change, frequent storm surges, rapid economic development and continuous population expansion have all brought tremendous pressure to the coastal ecological environment. A good coastal ecological environment is the foundation for human survival and sustainable development. Therefore, a comprehensive understanding and evaluation of the vulnerability of coastal erosion is of great significance for the sustainable development of the coastal zone. At present, there are relatively few comprehensive evaluations of coastal erosion vulnerability, and the methods have deficiencies. In order to further improve the methodological system of coastal erosion vulnerability assessment and make up for the domestic research deficiency in coastal erosion vulnerability assessment methods, this paper innovatively establishes three comprehensive evaluation models based on the existing coastal erosion vulnerability assessment methods and applies them to the coastal erosion vulnerability assessment in the coastal areas of Chinese mainland. This study integrates the fuzzy decision test and evaluation test method (DEMATEL), the Network Analytic Hierarchy Process (ANP), the CRITIC method (Criterion Correlation through Intercrieria Correlation), and the improved The MABAC method (Multi-Attributive Border Approximation area Comparison) is organically combined to propose a new comprehensive evaluation model. This model makes the calculation of index weights more reasonable by combining the subjective weights calculated by the fuzzy DEMATEL-ANP method with the objective weights calculated by the CRITIC method. When implementing the new MABAC method, the BM(Bonferroni mean) operator is used to aggregate the index functions to reduce the influence of the inherent interdependence among the indicators. At the same time, the shortcoming effect is considered to make the subjective evaluation more flexible. (2) Construct a comprehensive evaluation model that combines the Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process (FAHP), Entropy Weight Method (EW), and the improved fuzzy C-means clustering (FCM). On the one hand, the reasonable combination of subjective and objective weights makes the determination of indicator weights more reasonable. On the other hand, the application of the improved FCM method not only resolves the issue of unclear grade boundaries of indicators but also enables quantitative analysis of coastal erosion vulnerability. (3) by combining the BW method (Best-Worst), the TOPSIS method (Technique for Order Preference by Similarity to an Ideal Solution), and the ANN method (Artificial Combined with Neural Network, a comprehensive evaluation model based on artificial neural network was constructed. This model can not only simulate the quantitative evaluation of experts, but also avoid human errors that occur during the evaluation process. Through the analysis of coastal erosion vulnerability in the coastal areas of Chinese mainland, we find that the coastal erosion vulnerability in the Bohai Rim region, the Yangtze River Delta region and the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area is higher than that in other regions. In addition to the influence of human activities, regions with different erosion vulnerability levels all have their own regional characteristics.

Through the analysis of the disaster-forming conditions, disaster risk levels, and disaster-bearing conditions of the Haikou coastal zone, the fuzzy mathematics method was adopted to study coastal intrusion and conduct a comprehensive assessment of the risk of erosion disasters, determining the risk level and distribution range of coastal erosion disasters. The evaluation results were relatively satisfactory. The results of the risk and vulnerability assessment show that the high-risk areas of coastal erosion in Haikou are distributed along some sections of Haikou Holiday Beach and Changbin North Fifth Road. Partial sections of the banks along the Wuyuan River Estuary and the Nandu River eastward to Dayuan Village are also key areas for the prevention and control of coastal erosion disasters in the future. To prevent further deterioration of high-risk areas and maintain the normal production and life of coastal residents, the following measures are proposed: (1) Strictly prohibit sand mining near and along the shore. Competent authorities should strengthen supervision over reclamation projects to prevent new illegal and non-compliant sand mining at sea. (2) Strictly prohibit sand mining in rivers. Relevant government departments should take joint actions and accelerate the introduction of corresponding river sand management policies to prevent uncontrolled sand mining in the Nandu River Basin, ensuring the normal flow of sediment from rivers into the sea and preventing coastal erosion and retreat. (3) Actively carry out rectification and restoration work in high-risk sections of coastal erosion. Severe coastal erosion indicates that the coast in this area is already in a serious state of danger and cannot restore itself naturally. Relevant authorities should identify the main causes of coastal erosion and carry out targeted rectification and restoration work.

Haiyang City is rich in sandy coastal resources, stretching nearly 120 kilometers. It is particularly renowned for its 10,000-meter beach bathing area. However, in recent years, the erosion of the beach has seriously affected its social and economic development. This study is based on a continuous 3-year investigation of coastline erosion and typical profile erosion and silt on both sides of the approximately 20-kilometer typical sandy coast in Haiyang City. It quantitatively studies beach erosion and its changes, supplemented by a one-dimensional numerical model, to investigate the characteristics of sediment movement and beach erosion mechanisms under the influence of human activities. The research results show that 42.5% and 5.90% of the sandy coasts in the investigation area are in a state of erosion and strong erosion respectively. The erosion intensity of the eastern coast of Yangjiaoran is higher than that of the western coast. The beach erosion intensity between Haiyang Port and Haiyang Nuclear Power Plant is the highest. The beach erosion intensity is closely related to the degree of disturbance caused by human activities. The main reasons for the severe erosion of some sandy coasts in Haiyang City caused by coastal engineering, coastal aquaculture and artificial sand mining are that the erosion mechanisms on both sides of Yangjiaopan are also different. Based on the characteristics of the erosion mechanism of each section of the coastline, targeted countermeasures such as returning aquaculture to the beach, removing protruding embankments and beach feeding were proposed to mitigate the threat of erosion of the sandy coast in Haiyang.

To protect the sandy coast and its ecosystem of Yalong Bay, this article uses the monitoring data of 2016 and 2019 as well as the historical data of 2008. Based on relevant technical standards and methods, it comprehensively evaluates the erosion status and intensity of the sandy coast of Yalong Bay from two aspects: the rate of shoreline retreat and the rate of shoreline erosion, and analyzes the causes. The research results show that there is persistent and severe coastal erosion on the sandy coast of Yalong Bay. From 2008 to 2016, the rate of coastline retreat and the average annual area of land loss were approximately -1.00 m/a and 2,080 m ² respectively. From 2016 to 2019, the rate of coastline retreat and the average annual area of land loss were approximately -1.80 m/a and 9,836 m ² respectively. There is a significant intensifying trend of coastal erosion in Yalong Bay. From 2016 to 2019, the shoreline erosion rates of the eastern and western coasts of Yalong Bay were approximately -13.3 cm/a and -36.2 cm/a respectively. The shoreline erosion rate of the western coast was much higher than that of the eastern coast. The entire section of Yalong Bay is classified as having a strong erosion grade, with the eastern section being classified as having a strong erosion grade and the western section as having a severe erosion grade. The main causes of coastal erosion in Yalong Bay include coastal engineering construction, vegetation destruction and sea level rise.

Coastal erosion is the most representative type of geological disaster in the coastal zone, featuring slow onset and concealment, diverse types and forms of erosion, large spatio-temporal variations, significant spatial differentiation, numerous disaster-causing factors and complex disaster formation mechanisms. The occurrence of coastal erosion usually leads to the erosion and loss of a large amount of land resources along the coast, and many fixed coastal buildings and facilities are damaged and destroyed. Based on elaborating the connotation and denotation of the concept of coastal erosion, systematically summarizing the monitoring and early warning technologies of coastal erosion, and the basic characteristics of coastal erosion in China, this paper summarizes the research progress in aspects such as the disaster process and development trend of coastal erosion, the causes and mechanisms of disasters, disaster risk assessment and prevention and control countermeasures. In view of the severe coastal erosion disasters, complex causes, significant regional differences, and the non-uniformity of evaluation factors and method systems in our country, this paper looks forward to and proposes several development trends in the future research on coastal erosion investigation and evaluation. In terms of capacity building, an intelligent monitoring platform based on advanced technologies such as big data and cloud computing, as well as multi-source data such as dynamic sedimentation of coastal erosion, should be established to simultaneously obtain Marine data Data on the changes of multiple factors causing, generating and bearing disasters due to shore erosion can be used to accurately explore the causes and mechanisms of erosion and predict the trend of coastal erosion disasters. Develop the basic theory of the mutual coupling of coastal processes, dynamic conditions and sediment transport, research and develop rapid real-time monitoring instruments and equipment for the coupling model of coastal erosion disasters and the dynamic changes of coastal erosion disaster situations, and establish a scientific, operational and universal coastal erosion risk assessment method system. Accurately identify the main disaster-causing factors of different types and changing characteristics of coastal erosion, quantitatively estimate the erosion contribution rate of different disaster-causing factors, propose a scientific and effective coastal erosion monitoring and early warning system, and formulate targeted protective countermeasures.
